title: microlepia dubia var. subglabra
intro: Microlepia dubia var. subglabra, also known as the Himalayan fern, is a type of plant that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae, commonly found in the Himalayan regions of Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Microlepia dubia var. subglabra include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is often prepared as an herbal tea or tincture, and its extracts are also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
